faqs.question: How do I bet on the FIFA World Cup?
faqs.targetKeyword: how to bet on world cup
faqs.answerGuidance: Explain the process: choose a licensed US sportsbook (FanDuel, BetMGM, DraftKings), create an account, deposit funds, navigate to soccer/World Cup markets, select your bet type, enter stake, and confirm. Mention age requirement (21+) and that you must be in a legal state.

faqs.question: What are the best sportsbooks for World Cup betting?
faqs.answerGuidance: Recommend FanDuel for best soccer interface, BetMGM for competitive odds, DraftKings for live betting, Caesars for parlays, and bet365 for international soccer depth. Note all are licensed and regulated in the US.

faqs.question: Can I bet on World Cup games live?
faqs.answerGuidance: Yes, all major US sportsbooks offer live in-play betting on World Cup matches. Cover available live markets (next goal, match result, totals), how odds shift during play, and which sportsbooks have the best live betting interfaces for soccer.

faqs.question: What types of bets can I place on the World Cup?
faqs.answerGuidance: Cover moneyline (match winner including draw), spread (goal handicap), totals (over/under goals), futures (tournament winner, group winners), and props (top scorer, number of cards, exact score). Mention same-game parlays combining multiple World Cup markets.

faqs.question: How do World Cup parlays work?
faqs.answerGuidance: Explain combining multiple World Cup bets into one ticket for higher payouts. Cover same-game parlays (multiple bets within one match) and multi-match parlays (picks across different games). Note all selections must win. Link to parlay calculator for payout estimates.

faqs.question: What are the current World Cup 2026 odds?
faqs.answerGuidance: Provide context on current futures odds landscape. Note that odds will shift as the tournament approaches. Mention favorites (typically Brazil, Argentina, France, England) and host nation value (USA). Direct readers to the World Cup 2026 odds page for live updates.

faqs.question: Is World Cup spread betting available in the US?
faqs.answerGuidance: Yes, US sportsbooks offer goal spreads (also called handicap betting) for World Cup matches. Explain how soccer spreads work with examples: Team A -1.5 goals means they must win by 2 or more. Cover alternate spreads and Asian handicaps where available.

faqs.question: What World Cup betting strategies should beginners use?
faqs.answerGuidance: Cover bankroll management (bet 1-3% per wager), focus on group stage matches with more predictable outcomes, research team form and squad depth, consider draw odds in group stages (historically common), shop for best odds across multiple sportsbooks, and avoid emotional betting on home team.
